challenges.heading: Still figuring out where AI fits in your product?
challenges.description: If this sounds like your roadmap, you do not need more AI ideas. You need a focused plan.

cards.description: Everyone is demanding AI. Nobody agrees on what to build first.
You are shipping AI features just to keep up, not because they solve anything real.
You have too many AI ideas on the roadmap and no framework to choose between them.
You do not know if what you are imagining is technically possible with your real data.
You are guessing what users need from AI instead of validating it.
AI feels urgent, but you cannot justify where to start without a clear ROI case.
Sales wants one thing, product wants another, and engineering says it is too complex.

includes.heading: We conduct a focused engagement to de-risk your AI roadmap.

items.title: Interview your team (product, engineering, support, sales)
Audit your product’s workflows, data, and pain points.
Analyze where users waste time, make errors, or need decisions made for them.
Map AI use cases against real user needs.
Prioritize by impact, feasibility, and risk.
Deliver a clear recommendation: build this first, ignore that

includes.outcomeTitle: A clear deliverable: what to build, why it matters, and what it takes.

whoThisIsFor.heading: You are under pressure to add AI, but need a clear starting point.

cards.title: You are pressured to add AI quickly
cards.description: Board, investors, or competitors are pushing. You need a rational path, not reactive feature requests.

cards.title: You have ideas but no validation framework
cards.description: Your team has opinions and requests, but needs objective prioritization to separate signal from noise.

cards.title: You want speed without wasting engineering cycles
cards.description: A wrong AI bet can cost months of effort. You want confidence before implementation starts.

cards.title: You own product outcomes
cards.description: You are a Founder, CTO, Head of Product, or Product Owner who needs clear decisions, not more opinions.

items.title: AI Opportunity Map
items.description: Visual framework showing every potential use case for AI-native experiences in your product, categorized by workflow, user type, and value driver.

items.title: Priority Matrix
items.description: Each opportunity scored on user impact (time saved, errors reduced, decisions automated), technical feasibility (data availability, integration complexity), business value (retention, expansion, differentiation), and risk (trust requirements, failure modes, regulatory concerns).

items.title: Top 1-3 recommendations
items.description: Detailed briefs on the highest-priority opportunities covering what it is, why it matters, what success looks like, what it requires (data, effort, timeline), and key risks and mitigations.

items.title: Executive summary
items.description: One-pager for your board, leadership team, or investors. Clear, defensible, ready to present.

items.title: Walkthrough session
items.description: Live walkthrough of findings with Q&A for your team and stakeholders to align before moving forward.

process.heading: A proven framework to identify, score, and prioritize the right AI opportunities.

steps.title: Day 1 - Kickoff
steps.content: We align on your business, goals, constraints, market context, and what success looks like.

steps.title: Day 2 - Interviews
steps.content: We interview key stakeholders from product, engineering, support, and sales.

steps.title: Day 3 - Readiness Assessment
steps.content: We audit UX workflows and assess where AI can create measurable value.

steps.title: Day 4 - Opportunity Prioritization
steps.content: We map and score use cases by impact, feasibility, value, and risk.

steps.title: Day 5 - Readout and Recommendations
steps.content: You receive the final deliverables and a live walkthrough with Q&A.

pricing.description: Know exactly where AI creates real value in your product before you spend engineering effort.
features: AI opportunity map across workflows and user types.
Priority matrix scored on impact, feasibility, and risk.
Top 3 build recommendations with detailed briefs.
Do not build list with stakeholder-ready rationale.
Executive summary for leadership and investors.

whatHappensAfter.heading: Choose the path that fits your team and timeline.

paths.title: You build it yourself
paths.description: Take the deliverables to your team and start building with clear priorities and scope.

paths.title: We design it
paths.description: Move into AI feature design and get production-ready UX patterns, edge cases, and states.

items.title: UX Audit and AI Readiness
items.description: Before modernizing, identify what is broken and what is blocking AI in your current product.

items.title: AI Feature Experience Design
items.description: Design copilots, agents, and generative workflows optimized for real user adoption.

items.title: Legacy UX Modernization
items.description: Modernize outdated interfaces so intelligent features can be introduced without disruption.

whyUs.heading: The difference is in how we think, design, and deliver.

cards.title: 40+ AI opportunity mapping engagements
cards.description: Across fintech, HR tech, analytics, healthcare, and vertical SaaS products.

cards.title: Practitioners, not consultants
cards.description: Recommendations are grounded in real implementation constraints and UX adoption patterns.

cards.title: Fast and focused
cards.description: Two weeks, no bloated process, and no unnecessary workshops.

bottomCta.title: Ready to find out where AI will create real impact in your product?
bottomCta.subtitle: One 30-minute conversation could save you months of building the wrong thing.

faqs.heading: Reimagine your digital products with our redesign services.

items.question: What is AI opportunity mapping?
items.answer: It is a structured process to identify and prioritize AI use cases based on user needs, available data, and implementation feasibility.

items.question: How is this different from AI strategy consulting?
items.answer: Opportunity mapping is short and decision-focused. It helps you decide what to build first, while strategy consulting is broader and longer-term.

items.question: How do you prioritize use cases?
items.answer: We score opportunities on user impact, technical feasibility, business value, and risk to surface the highest-ROI bets.

items.question: Do you need access to our codebase or data?
items.answer: No. Product access, stakeholder interviews, and existing docs are enough to run this engagement.

items.question: Can this be done remotely?
items.answer: Yes, this engagement is fully remote with interviews over video and async collaboration.

nextSteps.text: Our UX Expert will collect project details and create a brief.
We'll prepare estimates and share a project proposal.
